meta.task: Adjudicate 33 contested Calc-2 question->concept rows; spot-check 10 scored-correct; compute corrected metrics; verdict on reversed-provisioning matching.
meta.inputs_verified: raw_section_chunks.json inspected per contested row to confirm math present-then-stripped vs never-present

cause_definitions.EXTRACTION_DAMAGE: text as given is under-specified; a sighted calculus expert reading the same stripped chunk could not reliably recover the intended concept either (confirmed against raw chunk that inline math was lost).
cause_definitions.MODEL_ERROR: text is sufficient and the model picked wrong or wrongly answered NO_MATCH despite a clear available fit.
cause_definitions.GROUND_TRUTH_DEBATABLE: model's pick is defensible or better than the section-derived expected label (cross-topic, calc-1-adjacent, or the concept genuinely spans two slugs).

task1_adjudications.reasoning: Summing a table of decade price-changes is discrete accumulation/arithmetic; model's NO_MATCH ('not really area-under-a-curve') is a distinction a human grader could also draw vs the near-identical rate-table problem 1.
Pure Calc-1 Mean-Value-Theorem-for-derivatives word problem shelved in the FTC section; none of the 40 Calc-2 slugs truly fit, so NO_MATCH is correct.
Toll-road speeding ticket is MVT (avg speed => some instantaneous speed); average-value-of-function is a defensible neighbor for 'average speed', expected FTC label is itself weak.
Kepler equal-area/speed question is grounded in the elliptical orbit; conic-sections is a better fit than the section's FTC label.
Perihelion/aphelion/major-axis average is pure ellipse geometry; conic-sections is clearly correct, FTC label is wrong.
Net-change-of-radius from a growing-area rate is solvable as pure geometry/algebra; model's 'related rates, NO_MATCH' is defensible, antiderivatives (net-change theorem) is the section intent.
Balloon volume->radius net change; same as 11, computable by algebra, NO_MATCH defensible, net-change/antiderivatives is section intent.

Braking-distance kinematics is integrating constant acceleration (antiderivatives). The slug physical-applications is defined as work/hydrostatic/mass, so model's pick is a plausible-English neighbor but antiderivatives is the taxonomically-correct label.

Raw chunk 62884-62887 confirms the specific substitution formula is stripped ('Use the substitution [MISSING] to express...'); for sqrt(1-x^2) the intended sub x=sin(theta) is literally a trig substitution, so model's trigonometric-substitution is defensible and the u-sub intent is unrecoverable.

Pappus cone-from-rotating-triangle reads as a plain solid-of-revolution volume ('does it agree with volume of a cone'); volume-by-slicing is defensible even though it sits in the moments/centroid section.
Sphere-from-rotating-semicircle is a disk/washer volume verification; volume-by-slicing is the natural read, physical-applications label is section-artifact.

Raw chunk 65464 confirms the velocity function is stripped ('velocity of [MISSING] after t sec'); without the integrand you cannot see IBP is needed, so model's FTC (distance = integral of v) framing is all the surviving text supports.
Raw chunk 66713 shows integrand entirely gone ('391. Evaluate [MISSING] (Be careful!)'); model correctly returned NO_MATCH at conf 0.05 - exactly right behavior on empty content.

PARTIAL: dv/dt=-g general solution is simultaneously a basics-of-DE and a (trivially) separable exercise; model put diff-eq-basics in secondary, separable primary - both correct, this is genuine slug overlap.

Raw 67366 shows the DE stripped, but surviving 'decay... half-life... fraction remains' is unambiguously an exponential-decay/half-life problem; exp-growth-decay is arguably a better concept label than the section's separable-equations.
PARTIAL: Newton's-law-of-cooling coffee problem is a separable ODE whose solution is exponential decay; model chose exp-growth-decay primary with separable in secondary - both valid, slug overlap.
drag proportional to v gives dv/dt = g-(k/m)v, which is first-order LINEAR but also (being autonomous, constant-coeff) separable; model's separable is a defensible solution method, not an error.
drag proportional to v^2 gives dv/dt=g-(k/m)v^2, which is NONLINEAR and genuinely separable - the model is actually MORE correct than the section's first-order-linear label.
Raw chunk 67854-67855 confirms the ODE and initial condition are both stripped ('Solve [MISSING] with the initial condition [MISSING]'); model correctly returned NO_MATCH at conf 0.1.
Recursively-defined monthly fish population is a sequence problem; model's exponential-growth-decay captures the proportional-growth modeling and is a defensible neighbor.
Monthly-compounded loan balance is a discrete recurrence (sequence); exp-growth-decay is a reasonable but slightly-off continuous-model neighbor.

P(coin flipped a multiple of 3 times before heads) = sum of (1/2)^(3k), an infinite geometric series - exactly the section's concept; model over-confidently (0.99) rejected it as 'discrete probability, not calculus'. Soft error: requires seeing the hidden geometric series.
Problem text literally says 'find the first eight terms of the sequence', so model's sequences pick is textually supported even though the section's accumulated-interest theme is geometric series.
Repeated-dose steady-state is a geometric-series accumulation; model's exponential-growth-decay captures the per-dose decay and is a defensible partial view.
Coupon-collector expected value n*H_n hinges on harmonic numbers (partial sums of the harmonic series); model's infinite-series-basics is a defensible read of the same harmonic-sum structure.
Jeep/scooter fuel puzzle secretly maps to harmonic-series divergence, but the distance formula is stripped (raw) and the connection is non-obvious even to an expert; NO_MATCH is defensible.

'Weights 1,1/2,1/4... what does this have to do with infinite series' is about geometric-series convergence/binary representation; model's infinite-series-basics is arguably a better fit than the section's comparison-tests.

Raw chunk 69900-69903 confirms the defining expression is gone ('Suppose that [MISSING] for all [MISSING]... for which values of [MISSING] guaranteed to converge'); intended ratio/root test is unrecoverable, model's low-conf sequences guess is honest.

Raw 70232-70235 confirms the power-series notation is stripped, leaving only the word 'sequence' visible; the radius-of-convergence intent is invisible, so model's sequences pick follows the surviving text.
Present value of a perpetuity is sum C/(1+r)^n, an infinite geometric series; model's infinite-series-basics is a better fit than the section's power-series-operations label.
Same perpetuity/geometric-series structure as 72 but text more stripped (present value gone); model's exp-growth-decay (interest/finance) is a weaker but defensible neighbor.

Estimating pi by partial sums of a power series to a target accuracy is a textbook Taylor-series application; model's taylor-series-applications primary is defensible/better than the section's power-series-operations.

Fully-specified clean paraboloid searchlight problem; model's own rationale literally says 'a geometry/conic sections application about a paraboloid' then outputs NO_MATCH at conf 0.98 despite conic-sections being in the list. Unambiguous self-contradictory low-effort slip.

rows.note: CO2 rate table -> approximate area under rate curve. Right for right reasons.
Explicitly about average value of f over an interval.
'Derive volume of sphere using the slicing method' - verbatim concept.
Work to lift a box = force x distance, canonical physical application.
Shock-absorber spring constant is Hooke's-law algebra (F=kx), no integration exercised; label matches the section but the problem is a degenerate pre-calculus warmup. Soft flag, not wrong.
Catenary y=a cosh(x/a) slope -> differentiating a hyperbolic function.
Explicit convergence/divergence of an improper integral.
Euler's method IVP - maps to 4.2 basics/numerical methods.
Carrying capacity + growth rate = logistic model.
Bounding a harmonic subseries above by a convergent series = direct comparison test.
Comparing radii of convergence of power series.
Whispering-gallery elliptical ceiling, major axis/foci relation.

task2_spotcheck.conclusion: 12 spot-checked (spread over all 7 chapters); all genuinely correct on their reasoning trail. Zero lucky/wrong. One soft flag (27) is a correct label on a trivial non-integration warmup, not a matcher failure.

baseline_primary_accuracy.note: raw, unadjusted, dominated by extraction damage + section-vs-exercise slug overlap

a_accuracy_adequately_specified.denominator_derivation: 79 total minus 6 EXTRACTION_DAMAGE rows (14,34,41,57,68,69). All 6 damage rows are non-matches, so no correct match is removed.
a_accuracy_adequately_specified.numerator_derivation: 46 original MATCH + 25 GROUND_TRUTH_DEBATABLE rows now credited (all 25 have model_was_reasonable=true; includes the 2 PARTIAL rows 43,50).

error_list.one_liner: Searchlight paraboloid: rationale names conic sections, then outputs NO_MATCH anyway despite conic-sections being in the list. Pure low-effort self-contradiction.
Coin-flip 'multiple of 3 before heads' is a geometric series (sum (1/2)^3k); model rejected as 'discrete probability, not calculus'. Genuine miss but requires seeing the hidden series.

c_coverage.note: The report's '11 uncovered' undercounted: it omitted integrals-exponential-logarithmic and alternating-series (both have zero problems) and wrongly listed integration-by-parts as uncovered (it has 1 problem, #34, but that problem is extraction-damaged so IBP is effectively untested). The confusable-techniques cluster (IBP, trig integrals, trig substitution, partial fractions) is effectively untested: 3 of the 4 have zero problems and the 4th (IBP) has only a math-stripped stub.

remaining_failure_modes: Hidden-structure word problems where the disambiguating math is implicit (coin-flip geometric series, jeep/harmonic puzzle) - low-effort reasoning rejects them as 'not calculus'.

Slug-overlap ambiguity that is a taxonomy question, not a model failure: separable-vs-first-order-linear, sequences-vs-exponential-growth, infinite-series-vs-power-series-operations. A single-label ground truth will always penalize a defensible pick here.

Section-artifact expectations: OpenStax parks calc-1-adjacent conceptual warmups (MVT climbers, Kepler ellipses, related-rates net-change) in Calc-2 sections; the honest label is often NO_MATCH or a neighboring concept, and the model gets these 'right' by disagreeing with the section.
task4_verdict.corpus_improvement_needed: Re-ingest document_id=5 with a pipeline that preserves inline/rendered math (MathML/LaTeX spans) for the confusable-techniques sections. The exact sections needed to test the 12 uncovered concepts - 1.6/1.7 (exp-log & inverse-trig integrals), 3.2/3.3/3.4 (trig integrals, trig sub, partial fractions), 5.5 (alternating series), 6.3/6.4 (Taylor/Maclaurin & applications), 7.1-7.4 (parametric & polar) - are precisely the ones the current pipeline strips to bare instructional stubs. Until math survives ingestion, the four-way IBP/trig-integral/trig-sub/partial-fraction discrimination (the whole point of a concept matcher) cannot be measured at all.
